← the Atlas

Multi-forge PR integration — the anyforge leaf

feature · seedling ·proposed ·JUN 09, 2026

Phased plan for kolu#1240 — phase 0a ships the gh log-noise fix, phase 0b extracts the forge-neutral PR kernel (schemas · poll loop · PrProvider · detection) into a new anyforge leaf as a zero-behavior refactor, phase 1 adds the Forgejo/Codeberg adapter. Grounded in a structural critique of the ic4-y/kolu#1 draft.

Contents

  • Phases at a glance
  • What the draft teaches
  • The anyforge leaf
  • The phases in detail
    • 0a — stop the lying WARN one-file PR, ships first
    • 0b — extract anyforge pure refactor, zero behavior change
    • 1 — the Forgejo adapter closes #1240
  • Decisions and open questions

The plan for #1240 — PR metadata on forges beyond GitHub. Kolu’s PR pill is hardcoded to gh pr view: on a Forgejo/Codeberg/GitLab remote it fails, gets misclassified as not-authenticated, and WARN-logs every 30s per terminal. ic4-y’s draft #1 · ic4-y/kolu#1 proves the feature end-to-end (open PRs resolve on a real Forgejo instance) and lands several right calls — but it grows the forge-neutral kernel inside kolu-github, so the Forgejo adapter depends on the GitHub adapter and even Forgejo’s error codes live in GitHub’s package. This plan re-cuts the same feature on the repo’s own precedent: anyagent is the neutral leaf agents share (packages/integrations/anyagent/src/agent-provider.ts:77-178); anyforge is the same move for forges. Phase 0b is a pure refactor (GitHub-only, zero behavior change), so phase 1 — or any future forge — is just a sibling adapter package.

Phases at a glance

PhaseGistUser-facing changeStatus
0aclassifyGhError: gh’s “none of the git remotes” refusal reclassified absent, not not-authenticated. One file + one test.Non-GitHub repos stop WARN-logging every 30s and stop showing a lying auth warning.first — ships alone
0bExtract the anyforge leaf: neutral schemas (renamed GitHubPrInfo→PrInfo as part of the move), subscribePr poll loop, PrProvider contract, sync detectForge; GitInfo.remoteUrl; registry-based startPrProvider with the single github entry.None — byte-identical behavior, every existing test green.pure refactor
1kolu-forgejo sibling adapter: Forgejo REST resolver (zod-validated, typed errors), Codeberg + KOLU_FORGEJO_HOSTS, popover recovery UX.Full PR pill — number, title, open/merged/closed state, CI checks — on Codeberg and self-hosted Forgejo/Gitea.closes #1240
laterGitLab adapter; per-host tokens; opt-in auto-detection probe.—not scheduled

What the draft teaches

A structural pass (Hickey + Lowy lenses) over #1 · ic4-y/kolu#1 sorted its ~1.5k-line diff into keep / re-home / redo:

Keep as-is — the parts a clean implementation adopts verbatim:

  1. The classifyGhError reclassification (with its tripwire test) — the actual bug fix, done at the right layer (classification, not call-site log suppression).
  2. remoteUrl on GitInfo — right home (kolu-git owns remote discovery), best-effort git remote get-url origin → null, gitInfoEqual extended so a remote change re-triggers downstream.
  3. The subscribePrResolver extraction — the poll/dedup/pending/emit-guard machinery genuinely is forge-neutral, and the draft’s Forgejo adapter correctly does not duplicate it.
  4. The registry idea mirroring AgentProvider, the exhaustive ts-pattern dispatch on PrUnavailableSource.provider (a new forge arm is a compile error at every render site), and the per-code popover recovery copy.

Re-home — right code, wrong package. The neutral contract (PrProvider, PrWatcher, subscribePrResolver, detectForge, the PrInfo/PrResult schemas) all landed inside kolu-github, so kolu-forgejo imports its own types from the GitHub adapter, and ForgejoUnavailableCodeSchema is defined in GitHub’s schemas file. The codebase already names this smell: the schemas header has carried a “when a second provider lands, promote the neutrals to their own leaf” note since day one (packages/integrations/github/src/schemas.ts:1-9) — the draft is the second provider, and it edited the comment instead of doing the promotion. kolu-common importing PrResultSchema from kolu-github/schemas (packages/common/src/surface.ts:48) is the same coupling on the wire side.

Redo — design faults, not placement faults:

  • Async detection races the git channel. The draft probes unknown hosts over the network (GET https://host/api/v1/version) and awaits that inside the git channel’s onEvent — but the channel contract is synchronous fire-and-forget, so two quick git events can interleave and a stale slow probe can tear down the fresh watcher. Detection must be sync and pure.
  • Auto-egress to arbitrary hosts parsed out of git remotes is a privacy decision smuggled in as an implementation detail — and it’s what makes the global KOLU_FORGEJO_TOKEN dangerous (a token sent to any host that answers the probe).
  • Lifecycle churn from interface shape. subscribe(repoRoot, branch, remoteUrl, …) bakes git state into construction, so the orchestrator grows a lastKey string and a teardown/rebuild dance just to survive a remote change; meanwhile resolveGitHubPr gained an unused _branch param mid-signature and resolveForgejoPr ignores its repoRoot.
  • Resolver rigor: responses parsed with as T casts despite zod being a dependency; HTTP status re-parsed out of error message strings (msg.includes("401")); branch matched by head.ref alone — the exact fork-PR bug GitHub’s resolver documents avoiding — and only state=open queried, so Forgejo PRs can never show the merged/closed pill. Zero tests in the new package.

The anyforge leaf

packages/integrations/anyforge/ (package name anyforge, mirroring anyagent), a leaf depending only on kolu-shared + zod + ts-pattern. It owns the three things that are stable while forges vary:

The wire vocabulary. PrInfoSchema (number/title/url/state/checks/checkRuns — already forge-neutral in shape), PrStateSchema, CheckStatusSchema, CheckRunSchema, PrResultSchema (pending | ok | absent | unavailable), and the closed PrUnavailableSourceSchema discriminated union including each forge’s code enum (GhUnavailableCode today, ForgejoUnavailableCode in phase 1) plus the reasonFor* / prValue / prLabel / prResultEqual helpers. Per-forge error codes are part of the neutral UI contract, not adapter internals: the client renders recovery instructions per code and must match(...).exhaustive() — the same closed-union trade-off AgentInfoSchema already makes.

The provider contract — a pure resolver, chosen per resolve. Simpler than the draft’s subscribe-shaped interface:

type PrGitContext = { repoRoot: string; branch: string; remoteUrl: string | null };

interface PrProvider {
  readonly kind: ForgeKind; // "github" | "forgejo"
  resolve(git: PrGitContext, log?: Logger): Promise<PrResult>;
}

The generic watcher subscribePr(providerFor, onChange, log) keeps everything subscribeGitHubPr owns today — branch-change dedup via prResultEqual, synchronous pending emit so stale info never lingers, the 30s poll, the emit-guard — but takes the git context through setGit(...) and looks the provider up on each resolve (providerFor(git) = registry lookup on detectForge(git.remoteUrl)). One watcher per terminal for its whole life; a remote-URL change is just a different dispatch on the next resolve. The draft’s ensureWatcher/lastKey/teardown machinery — and the race it created — never exists.

Detection, sync and pure. parseRemoteHost (one remote-URL grammar — SSH shorthand + URL forms — also yielding owner/repo so adapters stop duplicating regexes) and detectForge(remoteUrl): codeberg.org + KOLU_FORGEJO_HOSTS → forgejo, everything else → github. No probe, no KOLU_GITHUB_HOSTS: gh already resolves any GitHub-host remote it’s authenticated for (GHE included), and after phase 0a it degrades to a silent absent on hosts it doesn’t know. The gh CLI is the fallback prober — kolu doesn’t need its own.

The phases in detail

0a — stop the lying WARN one-file PR, ships first

classifyGhError: stderr containing "none of the git remotes" → { kind: "absent" }, slotted above the "gh auth login" substring check that currently steals it (the refusal message mentions gh auth login, hence the misclassification). Plus the table-test row pinning the string. Adopt verbatim from the draft. Verify: unit test; a terminal in a Forgejo-remote repo shows no warning icon and logs nothing above debug.

0b — extract anyforge pure refactor, zero behavior change

  • Create packages/integrations/anyforge/; move (not copy) the neutral schemas + helpers out of kolu-github/src/schemas.ts, renaming on the way (GitHubPrInfo→PrInfo, GitHubCheckStatus→CheckStatus, GitHubCheck→CheckRun, GitHubPrState→PrState) — the rename and the move are one mechanical commit. Gh* unavailable codes move too (vocabulary is neutral; classification stays in kolu-github).
  • Move the poll loop as subscribePr with the per-resolve dispatch shape above; delete subscribeGitHubPr and the GitHubPrWatcher alias rather than shimming (private monorepo, one test file to update). resolveGitHubPr’s public signature changes once, to PrGitContext — no placeholder params.
  • kolu-github shrinks to the gh adapter: getGhBin/resolveGitHubPr, classifyGhError, deriveCheckStatus/extractChecks, exporting githubPrProvider: PrProvider.
  • GitInfo.remoteUrl (packages/integrations/git/src/schemas.ts:8-15): schema field, best-effort resolution, gitInfoEqual, test fixtures — adopt from the draft.
  • startGitHubPrProvider (packages/server/src/terminalBackend/providers.ts:250-284) becomes startPrProvider: registry Map<ForgeKind, PrProvider> with the single github entry; onEvent stays synchronous. Since every remote dispatches to github (detection has no forgejo hosts configured by default), behavior is bit-identical to today.
  • Re-point imports: kolu-common/surface.ts and the 8 client files (prTooltip, RowPips, TerminalMeta, WorkspaceGrid, CloseConfirm, MetadataInspector, PrUnavailablePopover, terminalSubject) from kolu-github/schemas → anyforge/schemas. README package table gains the anyforge row.

Verify: every existing unit + e2e test green with no semantic edits (fixtures/rename plumbing only); no new env vars; no changelog entry — nothing changed.

1 — the Forgejo adapter closes #1240

  • packages/integrations/forgejo/ depending on anyforge + kolu-shared only — kolu-github appears nowhere in its tree.
  • Resolver over the Forgejo REST API (keep the draft’s endpoints: /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls + /commits/{sha}/status), fixed: zod-parse responses; a typed fetch error carrying status: number (classify by field, not substring); match head repo + ref so fork branches with the same name don’t false-positive; query so merged/closed states are reachable; honor port/scheme from the remote where derivable.
  • ForgejoUnavailableCode joins the union in anyforge — the compiler then walks the plan to the client: reasonForSource and ProviderUnavailableContent each need their forgejo arm (keep the draft’s per-code popover copy: token setup, timeout, not-found).
  • Tokens: KOLU_FORGEJO_TOKEN, injected as a parameter (not read from process.env inside the resolver), sent only to explicitly configured hosts — safe precisely because detection has no auto-probe.
  • Detection: codeberg.org built in; self-hosted via KOLU_FORGEJO_HOSTS (comma-separated), documented in the README the same change.
  • Unit tests for remote parsing, state/check mapping, and error classification; changelog entry (keep the draft’s); evidence: PR pill screenshot against a real Codeberg repo.

Verify: e2e on a Codeberg public repo (no token) and a token-bearing private instance; GitHub repos regression-checked; unknown forges still silent.

Decisions and open questions

#DecisionRationale
D1Closed PrUnavailableSource union in anyforge, exhaustive match in UIA new forge should be a compile error at every recovery-UX render site; mirrors AgentInfoSchema.
D2Unknown forge → github adapter, no probegh handles GHE; 0a makes everything else silent. Kills the async-detection race and the egress/privacy question in one move. Cost: one no-op gh spawn per 30s poll on non-GitHub repos — exactly today’s behavior.
D3Provider = pure resolve(git); dispatch per resolveNo watcher teardown/rebuild, no lastKey, channel handler stays sync. If a forge ever needs push/webhooks, widen the contract then — not before.
D4fj CLI not usedThe issue thread already found it rough; Forgejo’s REST API is one authenticated fetch and needs no binary on $PATH.

Open: per-host tokens (KOLU_FORGEJO_TOKEN is one secret for all configured hosts — fine while hosts are explicit, revisit if auto-detection ever lands); whether KOLU_FORGEJO_HOSTS should graduate from env var to a settings-UI surface; GitLab (different API shape, separate adapter — the union and registry are ready for it).
